shared_properties: The overlay follows the package palette and is authored for layered composition above world tiles and below UI.
Every mask region respects the fixed 16:9 camera contract and the hard-edge visual language of the project.
Transparency is intentional and must remain editable by shader tint rather than being baked into opaque color blocks.
differences: This asset defines safety readability rather than a tangible prop or persistent HUD element.
The overlay uses large compositional fields instead of object-centric silhouettes.
Danger edges are intentionally slimmer and brighter than safe-zone fills to prevent the mask from flattening the scene.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the shadow survival loop, mirror and prism puzzle rules, scale targets, HUD placements, and Bevy runtime constraints for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art deco tower mood, silhouette contrast, gold trims, and hard-edge lighting style.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load shadow_safe_zone_overlay.png during Loading and feed it into the world-space mask compositor used by the WGSL fragment shader.
runtime_notes: Author the overlay with separate safe-zone and danger-edge channels so the shader can tint them independently.
Keep the darkest safe-zone fill transparent enough to preserve architecture and character readability under the mask.
The asset should tile or crop cleanly to the fixed 1920x1080 gameplay camera without subpixel seams.

context.usage: Supplies the base world overlay that distinguishes safe shadow pockets from active light hazard regions during gameplay.

acceptance_criteria: Canvas is exactly 1920x1080 px with no non-transparent pixels extending outside the camera frame.
Safe-zone tint remains visibly distinct from the background while still allowing the player silhouette to read through it.
Danger-edge bands stay at approximately 6 px width in authored resolution and remain continuous across beam intersections.
